label: Narrow-shell 2-STOREY 2BR / 2 baths, SIDE-SPINE STAIR, HALL-LESS ground floor — the compact sibling of the _hall variant. The open great room (entry, dining folds in) is itself the GF circulation: the stair boards straight off it, the T&B doors into it, and the kitchen opens directly behind it. Second floor keeps its landing hall (bedrooms always door off circulation). Fits an 8x10 lot (4x6 m buildable per floor).

adjacencies.note: GF: kitchen opens directly off the great room — dining counter on the shared edge (2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass = 2.1 m min); band + stools live on the great-room side to keep the galley aisle clear. Render-only (counter_divider) — briefs may set dining_counter: false to suppress it without touching this topology.
GF: T&B doors into the great room — the privacy trade-off of dropping the hall; door-placement scoring pushes it to the least-exposed corner
GF: open stair boards straight off the great room (the classic compact PH 2-storey move); the great room IS the circulation, and the boarding constraint holds this edge to the flight's bottom end
GF: T&B shares a plumbing wall with the kitchen — single GF wet bay; kitchen keeps its rear service door
VERTICAL circulation edge: flight and stairwell opening occupy the identical rectangle on their floors (rect-equality in the solver, graph connectivity for validation)
2F: the landing hall must meet the stairwell at the TOP end of the flight — step off into circulation, never a bedroom wall
2F: master door off the landing hall
2F: rear bedroom door off the landing hall
2F: second T&B doors off the landing hall

soft_proximities.note: 2F: keep the bath against the rear bedroom's front wall — bath block buffers the bedroom from the landing

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: stair_run_geometry
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: ALWAYS-ON (the 99999 trigger): a straight stair's run is riser math the solver cannot derive — 3.0 m floor-to-floor at 15 x 200 mm risers needs 14 x 250 mm treads = 3.5 m. min_greatest_dim_m pins the run; max_least_dim_m 1.2 keeps the flight a flight. Applies to both stair rooms (same type), held identical by stair_vertical anyway.

notes: 2-STOREY narrow catalog topology — SIDE-SPINE STAIR, HALL-LESS GF
(authored 2026-07-19 as the compact sibling of
2s_2br_nw_side_spine_stair_bath_hall, which declares this file as its
fallback_topology with fallback_below_buildable_sqm: 28 — shells under
28 m²/floor auto-route here; see MULTISTOREY_V2_DESIGN.md).
Rationale: on a 24 m² floor (8x10 lot) a GF hall costs ~3.7 m² — 15% of
the floor — purely for circulation on an already open plan. Dropping it
hands that area to the great room and kitchen, and NEWLY enables the
dining counter: great_room and kitchen share a wall only in this parti
(the hall sibling's bath band separates them).
Canonical layout (8 x 10 lot, 2 m setbacks, 4 x 6 buildable per floor):
GF:  Front: [great room (entry, full width, counter at rear edge)]
Rear:  [stair | T&B | kitchen galley       ]  kitchen rear-pinned
2F:  Front: [master (full width)                ]
Mid:   [stairwell | hall2 landing | T&B    ]
Rear:  [stairwell | br2                    ]
Stair: straight, left wall, 0.9 m wide, >= 3.5 m run, 15 risers @
200 mm / 250 mm treads; boards off the great room, arrives at hall2
(stair_boarding / stair_arrival hold the two mouths to opposite ends).
Solver note: zone_balance_rooms counts the WHOLE upper floor (minus
stairwell) as the private wing. Without it this topology is provably
infeasible: the great room is forced full-width (it must reach both
the stair-boarding overlap and the 2.1 m kitchen counter edge) at
>= 2.22 m depth (aspect), and the kitchen is forced to 2.1 x 3.5
(rear-pinned + touching great), so GF public >= ~16.3 m² while
master+br2 alone top out at ~15.1 on a 24 m² floor — the default
bedrooms-only private tally can never win. Counting the 2F wing whole
(bath + landing included) is the architecturally truthful 2-storey
reading. The _hall sibling passes without this override only because
its hall eats 3.7 m² OUT of the public tally.
Trade-offs vs the _hall sibling (deliberate, per 2026-07-19 decision):
- bath1 door opens onto the great room (visible from the sala) —
standard for compact PH plans, mitigated by door-corner scoring.
- stair lands in the living space (noise) instead of a corridor.
- +~3.7 m² of usable area and a real dining counter in exchange.
Naming note: the `bath` token undercounts the program (two common
baths, one per floor) — kept consistent with the _hall sibling pending
the 2s naming-convention pass.
